title: FAITH in Small Business Act
plain_summary: This bill makes it easier for religious organizations and faith-based groups to get business loans and disaster help from the government. Right now, these groups often cannot get this help if their main job is teaching about religion. The bill would change the rules so that faith-based organizations can get the same business loans and disaster money as other small businesses. This change comes after court cases said it was unfair to deny help to groups just because they are religious.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Small Business.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Fair Assistance and Impartial Treatment of Help In Small Business Act or the FAITH in Small Business Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Ensuring Equal Treatment for Certain Faith-Based Organizations in Certain Small Business Administration Programs
sections.text: The Small Business Administration wrote a proposed rule on January nineteenth, two thousand twenty-one. This rule was called Ensuring Equal Treatment for Faith-Based Organizations in SBA's Loan and Disaster Assistance Programs. This bill makes that proposed rule into an official law that must be followed.
